item.title: Research repository ArXiv will ban authors for a year if they let AI do all the work
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: ArXiv is doing more to crack down on the careless use of large language models in scientific papers.

item.title: The offline desk gadget that actually got me to sit up straight
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Deep Care's $350 device is pricy, but it runs offline and helps you improve your posture and movement habits

item.title: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman takes charge of product strategy
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: OpenAI's latest shakeup comes as the company reportedly plans to combine ChatGPT and its programming product Codex.

item.title: $60B AI chip darling Cerebras almost died early on, burning $8M a month
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Cerebras Systems was 2026's biggest tech IPO so far. But years ago, it burned through hundreds of millions working on a chip many believed impossible.

item.title: Users turn to jailbreaking their older Kindles as Amazon ends support
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: It may be possible to jailbreak an older, end-of-support Kindle and continue adding books to it. But doing so carries risks.

item.title: RJ Scaringe has raised more than $12B across three startups and investors still want more
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Investors can't seem to get enough of RJ Scaringe or his ideas. Storytelling and communication are one of his superpowers, according to Jiten Behl, who joined Rivian when the company had just a handful of employees.

item.title: General Catalyst posted VC rage bait and it worked, especially on a16z
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Compulsive X user Marc Andreessen himself couldn't resist responding, many, many times.

item.title: A hotel check-in system left a million passports and driver’s licenses open for anyone to see
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The tech company that maintains the hotel check-in system set its cloud storage to public, allowing anyone to access customers' data without a password.

item.title: Silicon Valley’s vacationland needs a new energy provider just as AI is driving prices up
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Lake Tahoe, Silicon Valley's favorite vacation spot, is about to get hit with higher energy prices as AI drives demand for electricity.

item.title: Tesla reveals two Robotaxi crashes involving teleoperators
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Newly unredacted crash reports reveal some of the troubles Tesla has had as it tries to scale its robotaxis.

item.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Once users connect their accounts, they will see a dashboard of their portfolio performance, spending, subscriptions, and upcoming payments.

item.title: Power prices are up 76% on America’s biggest grid, and a watchdog is pointing fingers
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: The price spike is a reminder of a deeper problem: The U.S. power grid was not designed for the electricity demands of an AI-driven economy, and the gap between what the grid can deliver and what the industry needs is widening.

item.title: US orders travelers on Air Force One to throw away gifts, pins, and burner phones after China trip
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: While the summit appeared cordial, China remains a key adversary of the United States, given its advanced intelligence and espionage capabilities.

item.title: Runway started by helping filmmakers — now it wants to beat Google at AI
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: AI video-generation startup Runway is betting that video generation is the path to world models. And that being an AI outsider is an advantage, not a liability.

item.title: Even GoPro is pivoting to defense
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The action camera maker, like so many other companies these days, is looking to defense applications as it evaluates a possible sale.

item.title: Meridian Ventures launched a $35M fund with a focus on MBA-deferred founders
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: This new fund will back founders building enterprise technology in the United States. Meridian is agnostic, co-founder Devon Gethers said, noting that the firm has already invested in companies in fintech, logistics, healthcare, and of course, AI.

item.title: Osaurus brings both local and cloud AI models to your Mac
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Osaurus￼ combines local and cloud AI models in a Mac app that keeps users’ memory, files, and tools on their own hardware.

item.title: Indian Uber rival Rapido raises $240M at $3B valuation
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Rapido has driven its growth by enabling ride-hailing for lower-cost and more flexible modes of transport such as motorbikes and auto-rickshaws.

item.title: What the jury will actually decide in the case of Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Here's what the biggest tech court case of the year is all about.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: More than 50 employees have reportedly left Elon Musk’s newly merged SpaceXAI since February, raising questions about burnout, leadership changes, talent poaching, and whether liquidity events weakened retention incentives.
